text: The Empty Cushion
Find why a second cup is warm.
I didn't wait. I simply hadn't put the second cup away yet.
Say, "You fixed that for me."
Ask whether every visitor gets reserved seating.
Pour tea and leave the empty place open.
Say, "You could admit you're glad."
...You remembered the temperature. Sit down before it changes.
That is fine. Hand me the other cup.
Too hot. Move over; I will fix it.
I fixed a cushion. Don't make it important.
Reserved seating costs extra. You can pay after the tea.
...At least someone here knows when silence is useful.
If I were glad, I wouldn't need you to announce it for me.
Put the cup down. The stain under the donation box is moving.
Good. It stopped pretending to be part of the floor.
I'll take the last edge. You kept it away from the cups.
Back up. Losing an argument with a stain is still survivable.
If we're settling boundaries, let's settle the spell-card terms too.
Two seals. Fine. You remembered the terms.
Terms accepted. You can argue for a rematch tomorrow.
Your tea's cold. Leave it; I already poured another.
The path gets dark early. Take the lantern by the gate.
The guest bedding is aired and ready.
That's information, not an invitation. Don't make it important.
The Empty Second Cup
The cup was warm before I arrived. The cushion is no longer stored away. An empty place can be a kind of invitation.
